leaves.title: Freeze live contradictions and bootstrap artefact set
leaves.objective: Inspect the frozen law, live router surfaces, template pack, and latest closed evidence so the new post-Gate-205 pack starts from repo truth rather than from chat memory.
leaves.workflow_intent_assertion: This leaf is pure planning bootstrap. It exists before any doctrinal rewrite so the repo can lawfully move from no-active-pack state into one active governance-hardening pack.
ordered_actions: Read the frozen law stack, current router surfaces, active vocabulary authority, baseline packet/data contract authority, template pack, README, and Makefile.
Preserve granularity when choosing gate and leaf counts; keep bootstrap, doctrine consolidation, template rewrite, taxonomy hardening, and operator-surface alignment separate.
Write the contradiction report naming every material conflict that blocks truthful future execution if left unresolved.
Seed the new gates master, leaves ledger, execution log, scope note, and document-touch checklist from repo-specific truth only.
forbidden_actions: Do not claim that a later coding or evidence-execution tranche already exists.
Do not reopen the closed Gate 200-205 pack.
Do not change runtime code or packet contracts.
Do not copy the old zip-first template language forward.
expected_evidence: new Gate 206 pack quartet plus contradiction report and scope note
no runtime files under src/ changed
definition_of_done: The new pack artefacts exist and describe the repo state truthfully.
The contradiction report is explicit and actionable rather than implied.
leaves.packaging_requirement: GitHub branch/commit receipts are the default closeout evidence. Create a fresh full-history zip only if the operator explicitly requests backup or offline handoff packaging.
document_touch_surfaces: new Gate 206 pack artefacts only at first bootstrap stage

leaves.title: Route the repo to the new active pack
leaves.objective: Move the live router surfaces from no-active-pack state to the new Gate 206 governance-hardening pack.
leaves.workflow_intent_assertion: This leaf changes only the live routing truth. It does not yet clean every stale doctrine surface; it only activates the new pack lawfully.
vocabulary_terms_checked: latest closed pack retained as evidence
ordered_actions: Update repo-root PLANS.md so it names the new active pack, its leaves ledger, execution log, scope note, and contradiction report.
Update the canonical gate map so Gate 206 is the current active gate and the closed 2026-04-05 pack is retained as evidence only.
Ensure the leaves ledger state is coherent with the routed active gate.
forbidden_actions: Do not leave PLANS.md saying no active pack exists once the new pack is created.
Do not leave the canonical gate map calling the closed 2026-04-05 pack active.
expected_evidence: PLANS.md routes the new Gate 206 pack
canonical gate map names Gate 206 as active
execution log seed status matches leaves-ledger active gate
definition_of_done: The repo has one active pack again.
The routed active pack and the leaves/execution state agree.

leaves.title: Prove Gate 206 planning integrity
leaves.objective: Add or update the targeted planning proof so the new pack’s router state, contradiction handling, and state invariants are machine-checked.
leaves.workflow_intent_assertion: This leaf closes the bootstrap gate by proving the new pack exists and is routed coherently before any later governance edits begin.
ordered_actions: Write targeted tests that prove the new pack is routed and the leaves state is coherent.
Prove the contradiction report is present when material conflicts were identified.
Record Gate 206 bootstrap receipts in the execution log once the gate closes.
forbidden_actions: Do not rely on brittle historical-string tests that cannot tolerate later valid states.
Do not claim Gate 206 complete without targeted proof.
expected_evidence: execution log receipt for the Gate 206 leaves
active leaves state remains internally coherent
definition_of_done: Gate 206 has a green targeted planning proof.
The execution log can record Gate 206 as complete and Gate 207 as next.

leaves.title: Collapse PLANS.md back to router-only
leaves.objective: Remove diary-style and duplicated historical routing clutter from PLANS.md so it becomes a short live router again.
leaves.workflow_intent_assertion: This leaf repairs the repo-root router itself before touching the wider doctrine stack.
vocabulary_terms_checked: latest closed pack retained as evidence
ordered_actions: Strip diary-style historical marker piles out of PLANS.md while preserving the minimum live routing truth and latest closed evidence references needed for onboarding.
Prove PLANS.md remains a router and not a running diary.
forbidden_actions: Do not delete needed latest-closed-evidence references if later readers still need them.
Do not reintroduce a no-active-pack claim while Gate 207 is still being executed inside an active pack.
definition_of_done: PLANS.md reads as a router, not as a tranche history diary.

leaves.title: Separate behaviour authority from process authority cleanly
leaves.objective: Reduce AGENTS.md to the stable behavioural contract and let docs/06 hold the detailed workflow law.
leaves.workflow_intent_assertion: This leaf stops AGENTS.md and docs/06 from competing as overlapping process-law surfaces.
ordered_actions: Keep AGENTS.md short and behavioural.
Keep docs/06 as the canonical workflow-law surface.
Remove or rewrite stale gate-map prose that still labels closed packs active.
forbidden_actions: Do not make AGENTS.md the active router again.
Do not let the gate map keep stale active-pack labels after the router has been cleaned.
definition_of_done: Behaviour authority and process authority are cleanly separated.

leaves.title: Rewrite the template pack to match live repo law
leaves.objective: Update the template-pack documents so future planning packs inherit the current GitHub-native workflow model and no-active-pack/new-pack-required law.
leaves.workflow_intent_assertion: This leaf repairs the durable source of future planning structure rather than just fixing the current pack locally.
ordered_actions: Replace zip-first default language with GitHub-native execution-ledger language.
Keep zip creation available only for explicit backup/offline handoff cases.
Update the leaves template so its global rules match live repo law.
forbidden_actions: Do not leave any template surface requiring routine zip packaging as the default closeout path.
Do not loosen the one-gate-at-a-time or quartet-moves-together rules.
definition_of_done: The template pack no longer conflicts with AGENTS.md and docs/06 on routine execution workflow.

leaves.title: Prove the template pack encodes the hardened workflow
leaves.objective: Update or add tests so the template pack mechanically enforces the hardened workflow instead of relying on prose memory.
leaves.workflow_intent_assertion: This leaf closes the template rewrite gate by preventing the old zip-first assumptions from drifting back in later packs.
ordered_actions: Write assertions that the template pack encodes GitHub-native routine execution, evidence-only latest-closed-pack handling, and current router law.
Keep the tests tolerant of later valid states while still rejecting the stale zip-first grammar.
forbidden_actions: Do not hard-code brittle string assertions that would fail on harmless later wording shifts.
Do not weaken the template tests into smoke-only existence checks.
expected_evidence: green template-pack tests proving workflow alignment
definition_of_done: The template pack rewrite is mechanically defended.

leaves.title: Define active-vs-evidence planning taxonomy explicitly
leaves.objective: Document and route the distinction between active authority, latest closed evidence, and older historical planning material.
leaves.workflow_intent_assertion: This leaf clarifies how later readers navigate the planning tree without relying on chat memory or stale pack status prose.
ordered_actions: State explicitly what counts as active authority, latest closed evidence, and older historical planning material.
Keep the taxonomy compact and operator-readable.
forbidden_actions: Do not perform mass file moves unless the taxonomy itself proves a structural move is necessary.
Do not leave later readers inferring the taxonomy from examples alone.
expected_evidence: repo surfaces now describe active-vs-evidence taxonomy explicitly
definition_of_done: The planning/evidence taxonomy is explicit and truthful.

leaves.title: Decide whether physical archive restructuring is deferred
leaves.objective: Record a bounded yes/no decision on whether docs/planning physical archive refactoring happens now or is deferred behind the new taxonomy.
leaves.workflow_intent_assertion: This leaf prevents silent future drift by forcing an explicit decision instead of leaving archive restructuring as a vague later maybe.
repo_surfaces: scope note or companion taxonomy note if needed
ordered_actions: State explicitly whether docs/planning physical archive restructuring is in-scope now or deferred.
If deferred, state the trigger that would justify doing it later.
forbidden_actions: Do not leave structural archive changes as implied future work with no entry criteria.
expected_evidence: explicit defer/do-now decision captured in repo docs
definition_of_done: There is no ambiguity about whether physical archive restructuring belongs to this tranche.

leaves.title: Align README and Makefile with hardened workflow
leaves.objective: Make the operator-facing onboarding and build surface reflect the hardened planning/coding split and bounded proof discipline.
leaves.workflow_intent_assertion: This leaf addresses the human/operator front door after the router, doctrine, and taxonomy have already been cleaned.
ordered_actions: Update README onboarding text so it reflects the planning/coding split and the role of the router.
Update Makefile guidance or targets only as far as needed to support bounded proof discipline without destabilising runtime build behaviour.
forbidden_actions: Do not make speculative runtime or toolchain changes under the banner of workflow cleanup.
Do not remove make targets that still serve the live repo unless a replacement is proven.
expected_evidence: README and Makefile align with hardened workflow truth
definition_of_done: The operator-facing entry surfaces no longer conflict with the live governance model.

leaves.title: Write the slim active-repo cutover brief and next-pack boundary
leaves.objective: Define the exact entry criteria and handoff boundary for the later slim active-repo creation and substantive test-audit tranche.
leaves.workflow_intent_assertion: This leaf closes the workflow-hardening pack by handing later work a precise cutover brief instead of another loose brainstorming boundary.
ordered_actions: Write the slim active-repo cutover brief.
Name the exact successor work boundary: slim active-repo creation first, then substantive test-audit execution.
Close the pack honestly and route the repo to no-active-pack or to the exact next pack only if it is created in the same branch.
forbidden_actions: Do not silently start the slim active-repo work inside this pack.
Do not leave the next-pack entry criteria implicit.
definition_of_done: The repo can hand off cleanly into the slim active-repo successor work without relying on chat memory.
